page: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It is configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system features hyper-threading with 2.0 threads per core and a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 1008.0 GB of system memory, yielding 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage consists of a 2835 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX48mds_v2 demonstrates strong memory performance, with uncached large-block write memory bandwidth reaching 158.98 GB/sec, placing it in the top tier. Uncached large-block read bandwidth is measured at 330.82 GB/sec. Single-core processing is highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2338.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 2168.28. Multi-core performance is characterized by a Passmark CPU score of 70724.12 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 53295.74. The server also achieves 74.99 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 28908.89 in Passmark database operations.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21 GB per core provides substantial resource density for memory-bound workloads. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy parallel deep learning training, though it achieves strong CPU-based LLM inference speeds, such as 252.37 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model. Given the combination of high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, massive RAM capacity, and local storage, this server is positioned for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and high-throughput data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a 24-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It provides 48 dedicated vCPUs, 1008.0 GB of memory, and 2835 GB of local storage. The system features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector and matrix extensions. In performance testing, the server exhibits top-tier memory write bandwidth of 158.98 GB/sec and strong database execution capabilities, processing 74.99 million Redis SET operations per second. While it lacks dedicated GPU hardware, its high-frequency CPU cores and substantial memory capacity deliver strong single-core and multi-core performance. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale database hosting, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1008 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, designed for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high-frequency dedicated processors with massive system memory and local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
1008.0 GB of system memory providing 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core.
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier memory write bandwidth of 158.98 GB/sec.
Strong database performance with 74.99 million Redis SET operations per second.
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database hosting.
